public_functions.behavior_description: Initialize a 3‑phase inverter PWM on eGTM Cluster 1 ATOM1 with complementary, center‑aligned outputs, 20 kHz switching, 1.0 µs dead‑time, synchronous start/update, and a period ISR on CPU0 with priority 20. Implementation steps: 1) Declare all configuration structures as local variables: main IfxEgtm_Pwm_Config, per‑channel IfxEgtm_Pwm_ChannelConfig[3], DTM IfxEgtm_Pwm_DtmConfig[3], a single IfxEgtm_Pwm_InterruptConfig, and IfxEgtm_Pwm_OutputConfig[3]. 2) Call IfxEgtm_Pwm_initConfig to load default values into the main config. 3) Populate output[0..2] for three logical channels (one per phase) with: pin = the high‑side TOUT map macro for that phase, complementaryPin = the low‑side _N TOUT map macro for that phase, polarity = active‑high, complementaryPolarity = active‑low, outputMode = push‑pull, padDriver = a standard CMOS automotive pad setting. 4) Set dead‑time for each channel via dtmConfig[i].deadTime.rising = 1e‑6 and dtmConfig[i].deadTime.falling = 1e‑6. 5) Fill interruptConfig for a period event on the base channel: mode = pulse‑notify, isrProvider = CPU0, priority = 20, periodEvent = IfxEgtm_periodEventFunction, dutyEvent = NULL. 6) Configure channelConfig for exactly three logical channels using ordinal submodule channels: channelConfig[0].timerCh = SubModule_Ch_0, channelConfig[1].timerCh = SubModule_Ch_1, channelConfig[2].timerCh = SubModule_Ch_2; set phase = 0.0f for each; set initial duty as percent values U=25.0, V=50.0, W=75.0 (percent, not fractions); assign dtm = &dtmConfig[i], output = &output[i], mscOut = NULL; assign interrupt = &interruptConfig only on channelConfig[0] and NULL on the remaining channels. 7) Configure the main config: select eGTM cluster index 1 and ATOM1 submodule, center‑aligned mode, frequency = 20000 Hz, enable syncStart and syncUpdate, assign the ATOM clock source to FXCLK_0 and the DTM clock source to CLK0. 8) Inside an enable guard, check if the eGTM module is enabled; if not, enable it, then dynamically read the module frequency via IfxEgtm_Cmu_getModuleFrequency, configure GCLK to run at the module frequency (e.g., 1:1 divider using IfxEgtm_Cmu_setGclkDivider), set CLK0 to derive from GCLK with a 1:1 count via IfxEgtm_Cmu_setClkCount, and enable the required clocks (at minimum GCLK, FXCLK_0, and CLK0) using IfxEgtm_Cmu_enableClocks. All of these CMU steps occur strictly inside the isEnabled guard. 9) Call IfxEgtm_Pwm_init with the persistent channels array pointer to apply the configuration; this performs pin routing and DTM pairing internally and binds the interrupt callback. 10) Store the resulting driver handle, the persistent channels array, and initial arrays (dutyCycles[3], phases[3], deadTimes[3]) into a persistent module‑state struct so they remain valid after init. 11) Configure a board LED GPIO as push‑pull output using IfxPort_setPinModeOutput so the ISR can toggle it; do not force its state here. Notes: a) Only three logical channels are used (one per complementary pair). b) Interrupt is bound via InterruptConfig to the base channel; the periodEvent callback has an empty body, while the ISR function toggles a LED. c) No PinMap_set* calls are required; pin routing is handled by the output[] entries.

Update the three phase duty cycles by a fixed percent increment and apply immediately. For each of the three logical channels: if (current_duty + STEP) >= 100 then set current_duty = 0; then add STEP to current_duty. Store updated percent values in the module state and pass the dutyCycles array directly to the immediate multi‑channel update API so the change takes effect with synchronous update semantics preserved.
